title: Tarenna guelcheriana
intro: Tarenna guelcheriana is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Tarenna guelcheriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are consumed to alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
